title: ReadCapacityStatus
description: The current status of factors affecting the read capacity of a serverless index

state.description:
The `state` describes the overall status of factors relating to the read capacity of an index. 

Available values:
- `Ready` is the state most of the time
- `Scaling` if the number of replicas or shards has been recently updated by calling the [configure index endpoint](https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/api/2025-10/control-plane/configure_index)
- `Migrating` if the index is being migrated to a new `node_type`
- `Error` if there is an error with the read capacity configuration. In that case, see `error_message` for more details.

current_replicas.description: The number of replicas. Each replica has dedicated  compute resources and data storage. Increasing this number  will increase the total throughput of the index.

current_shards.description: The number of shards. Each shard has dedicated storage.  Increasing shards alleiviates index fullness.

error_message.description: An optional error message indicating any issues with your read capacity configuration
